title: Satellite imagery tracks PLAN carrier group and surface combatants in South China Sea
summary: Unverified satellite imagery circulating on Chinese social media shows the PLAN carrier Liaoning operating in the South China Sea with up to nine escort vessels, supporting ISW analysis of expanded Chinese naval operations during Balikatan 2026.
contents:
## Satellite Intelligence: PLAN Carrier Group Activity in South China Sea

### Imagery Analysis
Unverified satellite imagery circulating on PRC social media purports to show the PLAN aircraft carrier Liaoning operating in the South China Sea accompanied by three guided missile destroyers and six guided missile frigates. ISW-CDOT has not independently confirmed the carrier's location, and official PRC media outlets have not reported specifics.

### Geospatial Indicators
The PLA Southern Theater Command announced exercises in the South China Sea on April 28, citing Philippine "attempts to stir trouble." Separately, Global Times reported the Type 076 landing helicopter dock departed Shanghai for sea trials, potentially joining the broader naval response.

### Commercial Satellite Capabilities
The tracking of major naval deployments through commercial satellite imagery demonstrates the growing role of geospatial intelligence in monitoring military activities. SAR and optical imagery providers enable near-real-time assessment of force dispositions even when official reporting remains limited.

### Intelligence Gap
The reliance on unverified social media imagery highlights the challenge of confirming specific platform locations in disputed waters where official PRC transparency remains minimal. Confirmed indicators include the STC statements and the Liaoning's April 20 Taiwan Strait transit, but precise current disposition requires additional intelligence sources.

**Source:** ISW-AEI analysis, Global Times
